mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs that erode corporate margins, causing negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or the regime-specific coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity risk appetite, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of high-GDPC1 observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable employment forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-PAYEMS interaction yields positive 60-day returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive value over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 workers signals an abrupt economic slowdown that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of payroll decline events, or the threshold signal fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapidly improving capacity utilization and corporate profitability, driving positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in recovery regimes produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample validation.
